title: NOAA NCEI Significant Earthquake Database — Iran events with quantified economic damage, deaths, injuries, houses destroyed
source_org: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI), Natural Hazards / Significant Earthquake Database

files.name: iran_earthquakes_all_events_2026-07-13.json
files.original_name: API JSON response, no damage filter
files.notes: 200 Iran earthquake events from NCEI's significant-earthquake catalog (broader inclusion criteria: magnitude/deaths/damage thresholds OR notable event), most WITHOUT a populated damageMillionsDollars field (deaths/injuries/housesDestroyed still often present)

files.name: iran_earthquakes_with_damage_2026-07-13.json
files.notes: 36 Iran earthquake events where NCEI has a quantified USD damage estimate (damageMillionsDollars field populated) -- the economically useful subset

files.name: iran_earthquakes_with_damage.csv
files.notes: Flattened CSV of the 36-event damage subset, sorted chronologically, derived directly from the JSON above (same 36 rows, no data altered)

units: damageMillionsDollars = estimated total damage in millions of US dollars AT THE TIME OF THE EVENT (NOT inflation-adjusted to a common year -- NCEI documentation does not specify adjustment methodology per-event, treat cross-year damage comparisons with that caveat)
notes: This directly answers the project brief's natural-disaster-economic-costs bucket for ALL FIVE named earthquakes: Buin Zahra 1962-09-01 (deaths 12,225; damage $30M; houses destroyed 21,310), Tabas 1978-09-16 (deaths 20,000; damage $50M; houses destroyed 15,000), Manjil-Rudbar 1990-06-20 (deaths 40,000; damage $7,200M -- by far the largest in the series, houses destroyed 100,000), Bam 2003-12-26 (deaths 31,000; damage $500M), Kermanshah 2017-11-12 (deaths 630; damage $750M; houses destroyed 15,500) -- PLUS 31 additional damage-quantified Iran earthquakes NCEI has catalogued back to 1956 (Bastak, Lar, Dasht-e-Bayaz 1968, Kerman 1981 [$1,000M], Ardabil 1997, Bushehr 2013, Ahar-Varzagan 2012, Khoy 2023, etc.), giving a genuinely rich time series for a 'earthquake economic cost over time' chart, not just 5 isolated points. Cross-checked against independent secondary reporting during the hunt: World Bank's 2004 estimate for Bam was ~$1.5B (vs NCEI's $500M) and Iran's own government put Kermanshah 2017 damage at ~5B EUR (vs NCEI's $750M) -- NCEI's figures are lower than some other national/multilateral estimates in these two cases; this is flagged here rather than silently reconciled, since the two source bases (NCEI's internal historical-damage-database methodology vs. ad hoc post-disaster reconstruction-cost estimates) are not measuring exactly the same thing (direct physical damage vs. total reconstruction/needs cost). A World Bank narrative citation for the Bam $1.5B figure is separately recorded in SOURCES.md Round 38.
